name: Find-Package

options.name: -AcceptLicense
options.description: Automatically accepts a license agreement if the package requires it.

options.name: -AllowPrereleaseVersions
options.description: Includes packages marked as a prerelease in the results.

options.name: -AllVersions
options.description: Indicates that `Find-Package` returns all available versions of the package. By default, `Find-Package` only returns the newest available version.

options.name: -Command
options.description: Specifies an array of commands searched by `Find-Package`.

options.name: -ConfigFile
options.description: Specifies a configuration file.

options.name: -Contains
options.description: `Find-Package` gets objects if any item in the object's property values are an exact match for the specified value.

options.name: -Credential
options.description: Specifies a user account that has permission to search for packages.

options.name: -DscResource
options.description: Specifies an array of Desired State Configuration (DSC) resources that this cmdlet searches.

options.name: -Filter
options.description: Specifies terms to search for within the Name and Description properties.

options.name: -FilterOnTag
options.description: Specifies the tag that filters the results. Results that don't contain the specified tag are excluded.

options.name: -Force
options.description: Forces the command to run without asking for user confirmation.

options.name: -ForceBootstrap
options.description: Indicates that `Find-Package` forces PackageManagement to automatically install the package provider.

options.name: -Headers
options.description: Specifies the headers for the package.

options.name: -IncludeDependencies
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet includes package dependencies in the results.

options.name: -Includes
options.description: Specifies whether `Find-Package` should find all packages within a category.

options.name: -MaximumVersion
options.description: Specifies the maximum package version that you want to find.

options.name: -MinimumVersion
options.description: Specifies the minimum package version that you want to find. If a higher version is available, that version is returned.

options.name: -Name
options.description: Specifies one or more package names, or package names with wildcard characters. Separate multiple package names with commas.

options.name: -PackageManagementProvider
options.description: Specifies the name of a package management provider.

name: -ProviderName
-Provider
options.description: Specifies one or more package provider names. Separate multiple package provider names with commas. Use `Get-PackageProvider` to get a list of available package providers.

options.name: -Proxy
options.description: Specifies a proxy server for the request, rather than a direct connection to the internet resource.

options.name: -ProxyCredential
options.description: Specifies a user account that has permission to use the proxy server that is specified by the Proxy parameter.

options.name: -PublishLocation
options.description: Specifies a location for publishing the package.

options.name: -RequiredVersion
options.description: Specifies an exact package version that you want to find.

options.name: -RoleCapability
options.description: Specifies an array of role capabilities.

options.name: -ScriptPublishLocation
options.description: Specifies a script publishing location for the package.

options.name: -ScriptSourceLocation
options.description: Specifies a script source location.

options.name: -SkipValidate
options.description: Switch that skips package credential validation.

options.name: -Source
options.description: Specifies one or more package sources. Use `Get-PackageSource` to get a list of available package sources. A file system directory can be used as a source for download packages.

options.name: -Tag
options.description: Specifies one or more strings to search for in the package metadata.

options.name: -Type
options.description: Specifies whether to search for packages with a module, a script, or either.

args.name: -Name
args.description: Specifies one or more package names, or package names with wildcard characters. Separate multiple package names with commas.

description: Finds software packages in available package sources.
